## 2.1 Regulatory Framework — Statutory Anchors

The approval pathway rests on a three-pillar legal architecture:

1. **FSS Act, 2006** — Parent statute, Section 22 deals with licensing/registration.
2. **FSSR 2011 (Chapter 2)** — Lists standardized products; defines what falls outside.
3. **FSS (Approval for Non-Specified Food and Food Ingredients) Regulations, 2017** — The dedicated procedure for proprietary food approval.

> [!NOTE]
> **Non-Specified Food** = An article of food in respect of which no standards have been specified under FSSR. This is the legal synonym for "proprietary food" used in FSSAI documentation.

### 2.2 Eligibility & Criteria for Approval

The applicant must demonstrate that the proprietary product satisfies **all** the following pillars:

- **Safety Pillar**: Each ingredient must be a permitted substance under FSSR (positive list principle). No ingredient in the negative list (prohibited substances under FSSAI's prohibited list in regulations).
- **Quality Pillar**: Product must comply with general hygiene and manufacturing practices (Schedule 4 of FSSR — GMP/GHP).
- **Labeling Pillar**: Label must conform to FSSAI labeling regulations (FSS (Labelling and Display) Regulations, 2020).
- **Novelty Pillar**: Product must NOT have an existing standard — i.e., the approval is granted specifically because no standard exists.

### 2.3 Step-Wise Approval Logic Flow

1. **Self-Assessment & Pre-Consultation** — FBO reviews whether the product truly falls in the proprietary category.
2. **Product Development & Documentation** — Draft formulation, ingredient list with FSSR references, process flowchart.
3. **Lab Testing** — Get the product tested at an **FSSAI-notified NABL-accredited laboratory** for safety parameters.
4. **Online Application via FoSCoS Portal** — Submit the application on the FSSAI Food Safety Compliance System (FoSCoS) at https://foscos.fssai.gov.in.
5. **Scrutiny by FSSAI** — Technical officer evaluates the dossier.
6. **Clarifications / Queries** — If raised, respond within stipulated time.
7. **Grant of Approval or Rejection** — Approval letter issued with conditions.
8. **Product Launch with Mandatory Labeling** — Approved proprietary food must carry an additional declaration on the label.

### 2.4 Mandatory Label Declaration for Approved Proprietary Foods

> [!IMPORTANT]
> Every proprietary food approved under this regulation must carry a prominent label declaration: **"Proprietary Food"** along with the FSSAI license number and the approval number. This is a statutory requirement under Regulation 4 of the 2017 approval regulations.

### 2.5 KTU Formula Sheet / Regulatory Cheat Sheet

| Parameter | Value / Specification | Reference |
|-----------|----------------------|-----------|
| Governing Act | Food Safety and Standards Act, 2006 | Gazette of India |
| Primary Regulation | FSS (Approval for Non-Specified Food and Food Ingredients) Regulations, **2017** | FSSAI Notification |
| Application Portal | **FoSCoS** (Food Safety Compliance System) | foscos.fssai.gov.in |
| Testing Lab Requirement | **NABL-accredited** + FSSAI-notified | ISO/IEC 17025 |
| Approval Validity | **3 years** (renewable) | Regulation 3(3), 2017 |
| Standard Application Fee (Indicative) | ₹25,000 | As per Gazette |
| Standard Renewal Fee (Indicative) | ₹5,000 | As per Gazette |
| Approval Authority | **Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI), New Delhi** | Section 4, FSS Act |
| Mandatory Label Text | "Proprietary Food" + Approval No. + License No. | Regulation 4 |
| Pre-existing Standard Categories | 14 chapters of FSSR 2011, Chapter 2 | FSSR 2011 |

> [!WARNING]
> Fee figures are indicative and subject to amendment by FSSAI Gazette notifications. Always cross-verify with the latest FSSAI order before examination or actual application.

---

<!-- SECTION_2_END -->

<!-- SECTION_3_START -->
# SECTION 3: Step-by-Step Approval Process & Analytical Matrix

## 3.1 Comprehensive Approval Procedure — Exhaustive Stepwise Derivation

Below is the complete approval pathway articulated as a sequential analytical derivation.

### **Step 1 — Novelty Verification (Feasibility Analysis)**

The FBO must establish that the product is genuinely "non-specified." The verification logic is:

$$
\text{Is Product} \in \{\text{Standards listed in FSSR Chapter 2}\}?
$$

$$
\text{If YES} \rightarrow \text{No proprietary approval required; only FSSAI license}.
$$

$$
\text{If NO} \rightarrow \text{Proceed to Step 2 (Proprietary Application)}.
$$

**Engineering Logic:** This is the **binary classification test** — a product is either standardized (governed by existing FSSR) or non-standardized (requires proprietary approval). There is no middle ground.

### **Step 2 — Formulation Documentation**

The applicant must compile a **Product Information Dossier (PID)** containing:

1. Name and complete address of the FBO.
2. Trade/brand name of the product.
3. Detailed list of all ingredients with quantity (Bill of Materials — BOM).
4. Reference to FSSR provisions justifying each ingredient (e.g., "Sugar permitted under Regulation 2.3.1").
5. Manufacturing process flow with critical control points (HACCP-based if applicable).
6. Shelf-life study data (accelerated and real-time).
7. Product specification sheet (physical, chemical, microbiological parameters).
8. Label artwork as per FSSAI labeling regulations.
9. Testing report from NABL-accredited lab.

### **Step 3 — Safety Testing Protocol**

The product must be tested at an **FSSAI-notified NABL-accredited laboratory** for the following minimum parameter matrix:

$$
T_{\text{test}} = \{P_{\text{chemical}},\, P_{\text{microbial}},\, P_{\text{physical}},\, P_{\text{contaminant}},\, P_{\text{pesticide}}\}
$$

Where each $P$ denotes a parameter set. Typical test profiles include:

- **Chemical parameters**: Moisture, ash, pH, acidity, sugar profile, fat profile.
- **Microbiological parameters**: Total Plate Count, yeast & mold, coliforms, *Salmonella*, *Staphylococcus aureus*, *E. coli*.
- **Contaminants**: Heavy metals (Pb, As, Cd, Hg) per FSSR limits.
- **Pesticide residues**: As per Schedule IV (FSSR).
- **Additive safety**: Within permitted levels.

### **Step 4 — Online Submission via FoSCoS**

$$
\text{Application} \rightarrow \text{FoSCoS Portal} \rightarrow \text{Scrutiny Cell, FSSAI HQ}
$$

The applicant uploads all dossier documents, pays the prescribed fee, and receives an **Application Reference Number (ARN)**.

$$
\text{ARN} = \text{FSSAI-generated alphanumeric unique identifier}
$$

### **Step 5 — Scrutiny & Clarifications**

The FSSAI technical cell reviews the dossier against the following evaluation matrix:

| Evaluation Criterion | Weightage (Qualitative) | Pass Criterion |
|---------------------|------------------------|----------------|
| Ingredient permissibility | **Critical** | All ingredients must be FSSR-permitted |
| Safety test compliance | **Critical** | All parameters within safe limits |
| Label compliance | **High** | Conforms to 2020 labeling rules |
| Process safety | **High** | GMP/GHP/HACCP documented |
| Shelf-life adequacy | **Medium** | Adequate real-time data |

If $n_{\text{queries}} \geq 1$, the applicant must respond within **30 days** (extendable on written request). Failure to respond leads to rejection under "non-response to query."

### **Step 6 — Decision**

$$
\text{Outcome} = \begin{cases} 
\text{Approved with Approval Number} & \text{if all criteria satisfied} \\
\text{Rejected with reason} & \text{if any critical criterion fails}
\end{cases}
$$

The approval number is a unique alphanumeric code issued by FSSAI, valid for **3 years**.

### **Step 7 — Post-Approval Obligations**

1. The product must be launched **within 6 months** of approval (or as prescribed).
2. The label must carry **"Proprietary Food"** declaration visibly.
3. The FBO must maintain records and submit periodic returns as required.
4. Any change in formulation **invalidates** the approval — fresh application needed.
5. Renewal application to be filed **before expiry** to avoid lapse.

### 3.2 Approval Timeline Analysis

$$
T_{\text{total}} = T_{\text{preparation}} + T_{\text{submission}} + T_{\text{scrutiny}} + T_{\text{testing}} + T_{\text{query-resolution}}
$$

Where typical durations are:

$$
T_{\text{preparation}} \approx 30\text{–}60\,\text{days},\quad T_{\text{testing}} \approx 15\text{–}30\,\text{days},\quad T_{\text{scrutiny}} \approx 60\text{–}90\,\text{days}
$$

$$
\therefore T_{\text{total}} \approx 105\text{–}180\,\text{days} \, (3.5 \text{ to } 6\,\text{months})
$$

This timeline is critical for **product launch planning** in food product design.

### 3.3 Comparative Analytical Matrix — Proprietary vs. Novel Food vs. Standardized Product

| Attribute | Standardized Product | Proprietary Food | Novel Food (Novel Ingredient) |
|-----------|----------------------|------------------|-------------------------------|
| FSSAI Standard Exists | Yes | No | No |
| Ingredient Type | Listed in FSSR | Permitted but in novel combination/form | Ingredient itself is novel |
| Approval Body | State Licensing Authority | **FSSAI (HQ)** | **FSSAI (HQ)** with extensive safety dossier |
| Dossier Complexity | Low | Medium | High (toxicology data, history of safe use) |
| Fee | State license fee | ₹25,000 (indicative) | ₹25,000 + extensive testing |
| Typical Timeline | 30–60 days | 3–6 months | 6–12 months |
| Example | Packaged drinking water | Fruit-cereal fusion bar | Insect protein bar |

---

<!-- SECTION_3_END -->

<!-- SECTION_4_START -->
# SECTION 4: Structural Diagrams & Schematics

## 4.1 Mermaid Flowchart — Proprietary Product Approval Workflow

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A["FBO Develops Novel Product"] --> B{"Is FSSR Standard Available?"}
    B -- YES --> C["Obtain FSSAI License Only"]
    B -- NO --> D["Compile Product Dossier"]
    D --> E["BOM and Ingredient Verification"]
    E --> F["Send Samples to NABL Lab"]
    F --> G["Lab Issues Safety Report"]
    G --> H["Login to FoSCoS Portal"]
    H --> I["Upload Dossier and Pay Fee"]
    I --> J["Receive ARN"]
    J --> K["FSSAI Scrutiny Cell Reviews"]
    K --> L{"Queries Raised?"}
    L -- YES --> M["FBO Responds in 30 Days"]
    M --> N{"Satisfactory?"}
    N -- NO --> O["Application Rejected"]
    N -- YES --> K
    L -- NO --> P["Decision: Approved or Rejected"]
    P -- APPROVED --> Q["Approval Number Issued"]
    P -- REJECTED --> R["Reason Communicated to FBO"]
    Q --> S["Label with Proprietary Food Declaration"]
    S --> T["Commercial Launch within 6 Months"]
    T --> U["Renewal before 3 Year Expiry"]
```

## 4.2 Mermaid Block Architecture — Approval Ecosystem

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    subgraph Stakeholder["STAKEHOLDERS"]
        FBO["Food Business Operator"]

    end

    subgraph Inputs["INPUT ARTIFACTS"]
        D1["Product Dossier"]
        D2["Safety Test Report"]
        D3["Label Artwork"]
        D4["Process Flowchart"]
    end

    subgraph Process["APPROVAL ENGINE"]
        P1["Novelty Check"]
        P2["Ingredient Screening"]

        P4["Label Compliance"]
        P5["Decision Node"]
    end

    subgraph Outputs["OUTPUT ARTIFACTS"]
        O1["Approval Number"]
        O2["FSSAI License"]
        O3["Rejection Order"]
    end

    FBO --> D1
    FBO --> D2
    FBO --> D3
    FBO --> D4
    LAB --> D2
    D1 --> P1
    D2 --> P3
    D3 --> P4
    D4 --> P5
    P1 --> P5
    P2 --> P5
    P3 --> P5
    P4 --> P5
    P5 --> O1
    P5 --> O3
    FSSAI --> P5
    SLA --> O2
```

## 4.3 Lifecycle Diagram — Proprietary Food Approval Validity

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Application
    Application --> UnderScrutiny : FoSCoS Submission
    UnderScrutiny --> QueryStage : Queries Raised
    QueryStage --> UnderScrutiny : Response Received
    UnderScrutiny --> Approved : Approval Granted
    UnderScrutiny --> Rejected : Non Compliance
    Approved --> CommercialLaunch : Within 6 Months
    CommercialLaunch --> ActiveMarket : 3 Year Validity
    ActiveMarket --> RenewalDue : Month 30
    RenewalDue --> ActiveMarket : Renewed Successfully
    RenewalDue --> Expired : No Renewal Filed
    Expired --> Application : Fresh Filing Needed
    Rejected --> [*]
```

> [!NOTE]
> **Reading Aid for Students** — The lifecycle diagram maps the temporal validity of approval. The "RenewalDue" state activates at month 30, giving a 6-month buffer before expiry at month 36. Plan commercial launch strategies accordingly.

# SECTION 5: KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap

## 5.1 Part A — Short Answer Questions (3 Marks Each)

### **Q1. [KTU University Exam – Model Question, Module 4]**
**Define proprietary food. When does a product require proprietary approval from FSSAI?**

**Model Answer:**

A **proprietary food** is an article of food that has **no specific standard** prescribed under the Food Safety and Standards Regulations (FSSR), but is manufactured using **individually permitted ingredients and additives** as listed in the existing FSSAI regulations.

A product requires proprietary approval from FSSAI when:

- The product **does not fall under any existing standard** prescribed in FSSR (Chapter 2 covers standardized products).
- The formulation uses ingredients/additives that are **permitted under FSSR**, but the **product as a whole is non-standardized**.
- The product is novel in composition, formulation, or form, and cannot be classified under any of the 14 standardized categories.

The approval is governed by the **FSS (Approval for Non-Specified Food and Food Ingredients) Regulations, 2017**, and is granted by FSSAI headquarters in New Delhi, valid for **3 years**.

*(3 marks: 1 for definition + 1 for condition + 1 for regulatory reference)*

---

### **Q2. [KTU University Exam – Model Question, Module 4]**
**List any four documents required for the proprietary food approval application.**

**Model Answer:**

The four key documents required for a proprietary food approval application are:

1. **Product Information Dossier (PID)** — complete formulation with ingredient list and quantities.
2. **NABL-accredited lab safety test report** — chemical, microbial, contaminant analysis.
3. **Label artwork** — conforming to FSSAI labeling regulations (2020).
4. **Manufacturing process flow** — with HACCP/GMP/GHP documentation.

*(3 marks: 0.75 each for valid document)*

---

## 5.2 Part B — Long Answer Questions (14 Marks Each) — Module Internal Choice

### **Question A (14 Marks) — [KTU University Exam – Dec 2024 Style, Module 4]**

**(a)** Explain the **regulatory framework and procedure** for obtaining proprietary food approval in India. Discuss the role of FSSAI and the key regulations involved. **(7 marks)**

**(b)** Describe the **mandatory documentation, testing requirements, and labeling obligations** for an approved proprietary food product, with reference to the 2017 approval regulations. **(7 marks)**

#### **Model Solution for Q.A(a):**

The regulatory framework for proprietary food approval in India is anchored on the following:

**1. Food Safety and Standards Act, 2006** — The parent statute establishing FSSAI and providing it the power to regulate food products, including proprietary foods. Section 22 deals with licensing and registration of food businesses.

**2. FSS (Food Products Standards and Food Additives) Regulations, 2011** — Chapter 2 of these regulations lists 14 categories of standardized food products. Any food not fitting these categories is termed a "non-specified food."

**3. FSS (Approval for Non-Specified Food and Food Ingredients) Regulations, 2017** — This is the dedicated regulation that prescribes the **procedure, fee structure, validity, labeling, and renewal** for proprietary food approval.

**Procedure:**

1. **Novelty verification** — FBO confirms the product has no existing FSSAI standard.
2. **Dossier preparation** — Formulation, ingredient list, process flow, label artwork compiled.
3. **Safety testing** — At an FSSAI-notified NABL-accredited lab for chemical, microbial, and contaminant parameters.
4. **Online application** — Submitted via FoSCoS portal (foscos.fssai.gov.in).
5. **Scrutiny by FSSAI** — Technical cell evaluates the dossier.
6. **Query resolution** — If queries are raised, FBO responds within 30 days.
7. **Decision** — Approval with a unique approval number, or rejection with reasons.
8. **Validity** — 3 years, renewable; fee ₹25,000 (indicative) for grant, ₹5,000 for renewal.

The **role of FSSAI** is central — it acts as the regulatory authority, evaluator, and approval grantor for all proprietary food products manufactured, sold, or imported in India.

#### **Model Solution for Q.A(b):**

**Mandatory Documentation:**

- Product Information Dossier with complete ingredient list (Bill of Materials).
- Reference to FSSR provisions for each ingredient (e.g., "Sugar permitted under Regulation 2.3.1").
- Manufacturing process flowchart with critical control points.
- Shelf-life study data (accelerated + real-time).
- Specification sheet (physical, chemical, microbiological).
- Trade/brand name and FBO credentials.

**Testing Requirements:**

Testing must be conducted at an **NABL-accredited (ISO/IEC 17025) and FSSAI-notified laboratory**. The test parameters include:

- **Chemical**: Moisture, pH, acidity, sugar/fat profile.
- **Microbiological**: Total Plate Count, coliforms, *Salmonella*, *S. aureus*, yeast & mold.
- **Contaminants**: Heavy metals (lead, arsenic, cadmium, mercury) within FSSR limits.
- **Pesticide residues**: Per Schedule IV of FSSR.
- **Additive levels**: Within permitted maximum levels.

**Labeling Obligations (per FSSAI Labeling Regulations 2020 + Proprietary Approval Regulation 4):**

1. The label must prominently display the declaration **"Proprietary Food"**.
2. The unique FSSAI **approval number** must be printed.
3. The 14-digit FSSAI **license/registration number** must be on the label.
4. All other mandatory declarations (ingredients, nutritional info, allergens, FSSAI logo, batch/lot info, manufacturing date, expiry date, manufacturer address) must be present.
5. The label must NOT make misleading claims or unverified health claims.

---

### **Question B (14 Marks) — [KTU University Exam – June 2024 Style, Module 4]**

**(a)** Differentiate between a **proprietary food, a standardized food, and a novel food** under FSSAI regulations. Provide two examples for each category. **(7 marks)**

**(b)** A startup wants to launch an **innovative quinoa-based probiotic yogurt with ashwagandha extract** in India. The product does not match any existing FSSAI standard. Outline the **complete approval pathway**, including testing, documentation, and post-approval obligations. **(7 marks)**

#### **Model Solution for Q.B(a):**

| Aspect | Standardized Food | Proprietary Food | Novel Food |
|--------|------------------|------------------|------------|
| **Standard Existence** | FSSAI standard exists in FSSR Chapter 2 | No specific FSSAI standard | No FSSAI standard; ingredient itself is novel |
| **Ingredient Permissibility** | Must comply with the specific standard | Individually permitted ingredients in novel combination | Contains a novel ingredient not previously approved |
| **Approval Authority** | State Licensing Authority | FSSAI Headquarters | FSSAI Headquarters with extensive toxicology dossier |
| **Dossier Complexity** | Low | Medium | High (history of safe use, toxicology data) |
| **Example 1** | Packaged Drinking Water | Fruit-cereal fusion bar | Cricket protein bar |
| **Example 2** | Biscuits (FSSR standard) | Chia seed smoothie mix | Hemp seed protein powder (when hemp was novel) |
| **Typical Timeline** | 30–60 days | 3–6 months | 6–12 months |

#### **Model Solution for Q.B(b):**

**Scenario:** An innovative quinoa-based probiotic yogurt with ashwagandha extract — a non-standardized product.

**Complete Approval Pathway:**

**1. Novelty Verification:**
- Confirm product has no matching FSSAI standard (it doesn't fit under fermented milk products standard due to quinoa base + herbal extract).

**2. Ingredient Verification:**
- Quinoa: Permitted grain (under general food provisions).
- Probiotic cultures: Permitted under FSSR.
- Ashwagandha extract: Permitted herbal ingredient (subject to herbal regulations).
- Confirm none are in prohibited list.

**3. Dossier Compilation:**
- Product name, brand, formulation with BOM.
- Reference to FSSR provisions for each ingredient.
- Manufacturing process (fermentation + herbal addition).
- Shelf-life study data.
- Label artwork.

**4. Safety Testing at NABL-Accredited Lab:**
- Chemical: pH, acidity, moisture, protein content.
- Microbiological: Probiotic viability, *Salmonella*, coliforms, yeast & mold.
- Contaminants: Heavy metals (As, Pb, Cd, Hg).
- Additive levels within permitted limits.

**5. FoSCoS Application:**
- Login at foscos.fssai.gov.in.
- Upload dossier + lab report + label artwork.
- Pay fee (₹25,000 indicative).
- Receive Application Reference Number (ARN).

**6. Scrutiny & Approval:**
- FSSAI evaluates dossier.
- Respond to any queries within 30 days.
- Receive Approval Number with 3-year validity.

**7. Post-Approval Obligations:**
- Label must carry **"Proprietary Food"** declaration + Approval Number + 14-digit FSSAI license number.
- Launch within 6 months of approval.
- Maintain records.
- Apply for renewal before expiry.
- Any formulation change invalidates approval — fresh filing required.

> [!WARNING]
> **KTU Examiner's Valuation Pitfall Alert:**
> - Students often **confuse "Proprietary Food" with "Novel Food"**. These are distinct categories. Novel Food = novel ingredient (e.g., new protein source). Proprietary Food = permitted ingredients in non-standardized combination. Examiners will deduct marks for this confusion.
> - Many students **forget to mention the label declaration "Proprietary Food"** — this is a 1-mark loss pitfall.
> - **Wrong fee figures** or outdated regulations cited will cost marks. Always state "as per latest FSSAI Gazette notification."
> - Failing to mention the **FoSCoS portal name and URL** is a common omission.
> - Students often do not specify the **3-year validity** period of the approval.
